svg.text: You're pregnant! From this point forward, your body will do
something extraordinary — growing a new life from a single
cell. Some days you'll feel amazing. Others, you'll wonder
what happened to your energy, your appetite, or your ability
All of it is normal. This guide walks you through what to
expect, what to do about it, and what decisions are ahead —
Every pregnancy is different. This is not a substitute for
your provider's care — it's a companion to help you know
what to ask and what to expect.
Your body is building a placenta, increasing blood volume,
and supporting rapid cell division. That takes enormous
Rest when you can — don't fight it. Naps aren't laziness,
Rising hormone levels affect your digestive system. It can
hit any time of day, not just mornings.
Dry crackers before getting up. Small bites every 30
min. Ginger tea or chews. Prenatal vitamin at bedtime.
Hormonal changes can make your breasts swollen and
A well-fitted, supportive bra makes a real difference.

Increase fluids, fresh fruits, and vegetables. Chewable
papaya enzyme tablets after meals can ease gas.

Sharp or severe pain is not normal — call your provider.

Take advantage of this window — sign up for classes,
prepare the nursery, enjoy how you feel.
Most women start feeling movement around week 23 —
beginning as flutters and building to distinct kicks and rolls.

Increased blood volume and uterine pressure can cause
veins in your legs to swell.

Mild uterine tightening 2–4 times a day is your body
practicing for labor. Normal and not dangerous.

juice, not milk. Increase fiber to offset constipation.

Your baby pushes your stomach upward, causing acid
reflux after meals or when lying flat.
Smaller meals, more often. Stay upright 30 min after
eating. Lie on your right side. Antacids are safe.
Digestive slowdown and pelvic pressure make both
More fruits, vegetables, and water. Ask about a stool
softener. OTC hemorrhoid remedies are available.
Extra weight shifts your center of gravity. Cramps tend to
Bend at knees, not waist. For cramps, point toes toward
belly and try ice. Prenatal massage and PT can help.
Fluid retention causes swollen feet/ankles. Bladder
pressure can cause leaking when you laugh or cough.
Elevate legs. Increase water and protein. Kegels (50–
100/day) help with leaking. Sudden severe swelling —
